examples.title: Stakeholder Identification Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm for identifying and categorizing stakeholders in a software project
examples.code:
PROCEDURE IdentifyStakeholders()
 stakeholders = []
 
 // Internal Stakeholders
 ADD_TO_LIST(stakeholders, "Project Sponsor")
 ADD_TO_LIST(stakeholders, "Project Manager")
 ADD_TO_LIST(stakeholders, "Development Team")
 ADD_TO_LIST(stakeholders, "QA Team")
 ADD_TO_LIST(stakeholders, "Management")
 
 // External Stakeholders
 ADD_TO_LIST(stakeholders, "End Users")
 ADD_TO_LIST(stakeholders, "Customers")
 ADD_TO_LIST(stakeholders, "Suppliers")
 ADD_TO_LIST(stakeholders, "Regulators")
 
 RETURN stakeholders
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates basic stakeholder identification by categorizing them into internal and external groups commonly found in software projects.

examples.title: Stakeholder Classification using Power/Interest Grid
examples.description: Algorithm to classify stakeholders based on their power and interest levels
examples.code:
FUNCTION ClassifyStakeholder(power, interest)
 IF power == HIGH AND interest == HIGH THEN
 RETURN "Manage Closely"
 ELSE IF power == HIGH AND interest == LOW THEN
 RETURN "Keep Satisfied"
 ELSE IF power == LOW AND interest == HIGH THEN
 RETURN "Keep Informed"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Monitor"
 END IF
END FUNCTION

// Example usage
CLASSIFY_STAKEHOLDER(8, 9) // Returns "Manage Closely"
CLASSIFY_STAKEHOLDER(9, 3) // Returns "Keep Satisfied"
CLASSIFY_STAKEHOLDER(2, 8) // Returns "Keep Informed"
CLASSIFY_STAKEHOLDER(1, 2) // Returns "Monitor"
examples.explanation: This pseudocode implements the power/interest grid classification, helping project managers determine appropriate engagement strategies for each stakeholder.

examples.title: Stakeholder Engagement Priority Calculator
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate engagement priority score for stakeholders
examples.code:
PROCEDURE CalculatePriority(stakeholder)
 // Factors: influence (1-10), interest (1-10), impact (1-10)
 influence = stakeholder.influence
 interest = stakeholder.interest 
 impact = stakeholder.impact
 
 // Priority Score Calculation
 priorityScore = (influence * 0.4) + (interest * 0.3) + (impact * 0.3)
 
 // Assign Priority Level
 IF priorityScore >= 8 THEN
 RETURN "HIGH PRIORITY"
 ELSE IF priorityScore >= 5 THEN
 RETURN "MEDIUM PRIORITY"
 ELSE
 RETURN "LOW PRIORITY"
 END IF
END PROCEDURE

// Example: Sponsor with influence=9, interest=8, impact=9
// Score = (9*0.4) + (8*0.3) + (9*0.3) = 8.5 -> HIGH PRIORITY
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates a weighted priority score for stakeholders based on their influence, interest, and project impact, helping prioritize engagement efforts.
